fault_hypothesis: Lines 76-77 incorrectly remove a duplicate from the merged result when both streams end with the same value. This post-processing pop is wrong and should not exist.

constraints.intent: After the pop, merged length should still equal sum of both input lengths, which it won't when a valid duplicate is removed.
After the conditional pop branch executes, merged should not be shorter than the combined inputs minus one — but the pop incorrectly removes a needed element.
Before returning, the merged list length must equal the sum of both input lengths since all elements should be retained.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
